sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Brighton & Hove Albion have shown decent form in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, scoring consistently. Liverpool, despite a recent loss and draw, remain dominant with 3 wins in their last 5, showcasing their attacking prowess led by Mohamed Salah.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Liverpool have emerged victorious with close scores of 2-1 and 3-2, indicating competitive games. Both teams have scored in these encounters, with over 2.5 goals in each match.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Liverpool lead the league with 83 points and a strong attack (83 goals scored), while Brighton sit at 9th with 55 points and a balanced but less potent offense (59 goals). Liverpool's superior expected goals and big chances created suggest they hold an edge offensively.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Brighton's Danny Welbeck and Kaoru Mitoma will be crucial for their attacking output, while Liverpool rely heavily on Salah's league-leading goals and assists. Liverpool's pressing and final third entries (as seen in stats) could exploit Brighton's defensive vulnerabilities.
